document_title: Recommended Reading Materials on Artificial Intelligence and AI Literacy
section_title: AI Literacy and Disciplinary Reading List – Classified by Education Level and Major Type

readings.title: Artificial Intelligence: A Guide for Thinking Humans
readings.author: Melanie Mitchell
readings.summary: An accessible and humorous introduction to AI concepts and history, balancing optimism with realistic limitations of AI capabilities.
readings.recommendation: Excellent for newcomers who want conceptual clarity without technical formulas.

readings.title: The Myth of Artificial Intelligence: Why Computers Can’t Think the Way We Do
readings.author: Erik J. Larson
readings.summary: Challenges the belief that AI will reach human-level intelligence, explaining cognitive and philosophical limits of current technology.
readings.recommendation: Ideal for readers skeptical of AI hype and interested in philosophical discussion.

readings.title: Myten om maskinene
readings.author: Morten Goodwin
readings.summary: A Norwegian introduction debunking myths about AI through accessible stories and humor, offering a local perspective.
readings.recommendation: Perfect for Nordic readers seeking approachable, non-technical AI insight.

readings.title: Maskiner som tenker: algoritmenes hemmeligheter og veien til kunstig intelligens
readings.author: Inga Strümke
readings.summary: A best-selling, highly readable overview of AI’s history and present, explaining concepts from neural networks to ChatGPT.
readings.recommendation: Recommended for all audiences as a national reference text on AI.

readings.title: The Master Algorithm: How the Quest for the Ultimate Learning Machine Will Change Our World
readings.author: Pedro Domingos
readings.summary: Introduces the five 'tribes' of machine learning and argues for a unifying algorithm. Explains AI’s broad methods for non-experts.
readings.recommendation: Useful bridge text between conceptual understanding and technical interest.

readings.title: Artificial Intelligence: Structures and Strategies for Complex Problem Solving (6th ed.)
readings.author: George Luger
readings.summary: Classic introductory AI textbook covering historical foundations and basic techniques, recommended by Norwegian AI faculty.
readings.recommendation: Good for self-learners seeking a textbook-style introduction without heavy math.

readings.title: Human Compatible: Artificial Intelligence and the Problem of Control
readings.author: Stuart J. Russell
readings.summary: A leading AI scientist discusses existential risks and proposes value-aligned AI systems for human–machine coexistence.
readings.recommendation: Essential for readers interested in AI ethics and governance.

readings.title: Deep Learning
readings.author: Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio, and Aaron Courville
readings.summary: Comprehensive academic text introducing neural networks and deep learning foundations.
readings.recommendation: Standard graduate-level text for computer science and data science students.

readings.title: Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction
readings.author: Richard S. Sutton and Andrew G. Barto
readings.summary: Defines the core algorithms and theories behind reinforcement learning from classical to modern approaches.
readings.recommendation: Recommended for advanced students exploring machine learning and decision systems.

readings.title: Intelligence Emerging: Adaptivity and Search in Evolving Neural Systems
readings.author: Keith Downing
readings.summary: Explores biologically inspired neural computation, bridging AI and cognitive science.
readings.recommendation: Ideal for students interested in bio-inspired AI or neural network theory.

readings.title: Gradient Expectations
readings.author: Keith Downing
readings.summary: Maps the origins and anatomy of natural and artificial neural networks, integrating insights from neuroscience and AI.
readings.recommendation: A cross-disciplinary look at neural computation for advanced learners.

readings.title: Kunstig intelligens redder liv – AI er legenes nye superkrefter
readings.author: Ishita Barua
readings.summary: Explains how AI tools can improve healthcare delivery and clinical decision support, written by a physician-researcher.
readings.recommendation: Relevant for healthcare professionals, policymakers, and students in health informatics.

readings.title: Artificial Intelligence Literacy in Higher Education: Theory and Practice from a European Perspective
readings.author: Imre Fekete
readings.summary: Analyzes AI literacy frameworks and teaching practices across European higher education institutions.
readings.recommendation: Essential for university educators and AI policy developers.

chunk_text.major_type: AI in Education and Literacy Development

readings.title: Kunstig intelligens i skriveopplæringen
readings.author: Agnar Lirhus
readings.summary: Explores how teachers can teach writing in the age of generative AI, offering theory and classroom strategies for KI-literacy.
readings.recommendation: Highly practical guide for teachers integrating AI tools responsibly in language education.

readings.title: AI Literacy in K–16 Classrooms
readings.author: Davy Tsz Kit Ng, Samuel Kai Wah Chu, Maggie Shen Qiao, Maggie Jiahong Su, Jac Ka Lok Leung, and Iris Heung Yue Yim
readings.summary: Provides research-based frameworks and design principles for teaching and learning AI at school levels K–16.
readings.recommendation: Crucial for educators developing AI curricula in schools and teacher training programs.
